title: Nintendo Switch 2 Price Hike to $499.99 September 2026 | Star Fox June 25 and Multiple Unannounced H2 Titles Confirmed
subtitle: Nintendo president Shuntaro Furukawa confirmed a $50 price increase to $499.99 effective September 1, 2026, citing permanent rises in memory costs, exchange rates, and oil prices. Star Fox arrives June 25 as a full remake with Joy-Con 2 mouse mode. Multiple unannounced H2 titles are confirmed.
subject: Nintendo Switch 2
description: Nintendo confirms Switch 2 rises from $449.99 to $499.99 on September 1, 2026 in North America and Europe. Star Fox launches June 25. Furukawa confirms multiple unannounced H2 2026 titles. Switch 2 adoption rate exceeds original Switch. Nintendo stock fell 8% on the announcement.

Nintendo Investor Relations — Fiscal Year 2026 Q4 Earnings Briefing

timeline.description: Nintendo Switch 2 launches globally at $449.99. Mario Kart World bundle available at $499.99. Day-one adoption exceeds original Switch 1 launch metrics according to Nintendo's internal tracking.
Yoshi and the Mysterious Book releases on Nintendo Switch 2, continuing the platform's early software lineup ahead of the Star Fox launch.

Nintendo president Shuntaro Furukawa confirms Switch 2 price increase to $499.99 effective September 1, 2026 in North America and Europe. ¥49,980 to ¥59,980 in Japan effective May 25. Multiple unannounced H2 titles confirmed. Nintendo stock falls 8% to its lowest point since August 2024.
Star Fox launches on Nintendo Switch 2 as a full remake with enhanced visuals, online multiplayer, and Joy-Con 2 mouse mode support. Pre-orders are open at Nintendo.com and major retailers.
Rhythm Heaven Groove releases on Nintendo Switch 2 and original Switch, broadening the addressable market across both console generations.
Splatoon Raiders launches exclusively on Nintendo Switch 2, the first major new entry in the Splatoon franchise built ground-up for the new hardware.
Nintendo Switch 2 standard price officially rises to $499.99 in North America and Europe. Consumers who purchased before this date locked in the $449.99 price point.
Fire Emblem Fortune's Weave and multiple additional unannounced titles release in the second half of fiscal 2026. Nintendo commits to full title reveal cadence at appropriate times.

newsletter_description: Every confirmed title, pricing update, and hardware detail from OzoneNews's dedicated Switch 2 team.
content_html:
Direct Answer

Nintendo president Shuntaro Furukawa confirmed the Nintendo Switch 2 will rise from $449.99 to $499.99 effective September 1, 2026 in North America and Europe, citing permanent increases in memory component costs, exchange rates, and oil prices. Star Fox launches June 25, 2026 as a full remake with Joy-Con 2 mouse mode support. Furukawa confirmed multiple additional unannounced titles for the second half of fiscal 2026. Nintendo stock fell 8% on the announcement to its lowest level since August 2024.

Key Takeaways

  • Nintendo Switch 2 price rises $449.99 to $499.99 on September 1, 2026 in North America and Europe.
  • Japan price increases from ¥49,980 to ¥59,980 effective May 25, 2026.
  • Star Fox launches June 25, 2026, as a full remake with enhanced visuals, multiplayer, and Joy-Con 2 mouse mode.
  • Furukawa confirmed multiple unannounced major and non-major titles for H2 fiscal 2026, with details at appropriate times.
  • Switch 2 adoption rate is higher than original Switch at equivalent launch period. Pokemon Pokopia cited as primary driver.
  • Nintendo stock fell 8% on the price announcement, reaching its lowest point since August 2024.

Nintendo president Shuntaro Furukawa announced on May 11, 2026 that the Nintendo Switch 2 standard price will increase from $449.99 to $499.99 effective September 1, 2026 in North America and Europe. The Japan domestic price moves from ¥49,980 to ¥59,980 on May 25, reflecting a more immediate regional adjustment.

Furukawa attributed the increase to three permanent cost pressures: rising memory component prices affecting both NAND flash and DRAM markets, unfavorable exchange rate movements between the yen and the dollar and euro, and elevated oil prices increasing logistics and manufacturing costs across the supply chain. The statement framed these as structural rather than temporary conditions, signaling Nintendo does not expect costs to revert to prior levels.

The timing reflects Nintendo's initial strategy of loss-leading hardware pricing to maximize adoption at launch, followed by a profitability correction once install base momentum is established. Switch 2 launched June 5, 2026 at $449.99. The September 1 increase gives consumers a 13-week window to purchase at the lower price point before the revision takes effect.

Star Fox launches June 25, 2026 on Nintendo Switch 2 as a full remake, not a port or remaster. The title features rebuilt visuals at native resolution in docked mode via DLSS upscaling, expanded online multiplayer modes, and full Joy-Con 2 mouse mode support, which maps the gyroscope and new click surface to aiming and menu navigation.

The release arrives three weeks after the June 5, 2026 console launch, filling a deliberate gap in the early software lineup between the launch window titles and the July releases. Nintendo Switch 2 launch games included Mario Kart World and Donkey Kong Bananza, with Star Fox positioned as the first major third-week release.

Pre-orders for Star Fox are open now at Nintendo.com and major retail partners. No special edition or bundle has been announced alongside the standard title, though Nintendo has indicated Joy-Con 2 accessories will be positioned alongside the launch.

 The $50 price increase does not apply retroactively. Consumers who purchase Nintendo Switch 2 at any authorized retailer before September 1, 2026 retain the $449.99 price point. The pre-order guide covers current availability at Nintendo.com, Best Buy, GameStop, Amazon, Target, and Walmart.

Furukawa confirmed that Nintendo has both major and non-major titles in development for the second half of fiscal year 2026, with full details to be disclosed at appropriate times. This language is consistent with Nintendo's standard pre-Direct communication cadence, suggesting a Nintendo Direct is planned for mid-summer 2026 to reveal the H2 slate.

The currently confirmed release schedule for 2026 covers six titles across different genres and audiences, establishing a consistent monthly release cadence from May through the second half of the year:

  • May 21, 2026 — Yoshi and the Mysterious Book (Switch 2 exclusive, platformer)
  • June 25, 2026 — Star Fox (Switch 2 exclusive, full remake, action)
  • July 2, 2026 — Rhythm Heaven Groove (Switch 2 and original Switch, rhythm)
  • July 23, 2026 — Splatoon Raiders (Switch 2 exclusive, multiplayer action)
  • Late 2026 — Fire Emblem Fortune's Weave (Switch 2, date TBD)
  • H2 2026 — Multiple unannounced major and non-major titles (details at appropriate time)

Industry speculation points to a potential The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remaster or remake among the unannounced slate, based on trademark filings and developer signals. Nintendo has not confirmed or denied this. OzoneNews will update this article when official announcements are made.

Furukawa confirmed during the investor briefing that Nintendo Switch 2 adoption rates are tracking higher than the original Switch at the equivalent post-launch period. The primary driver cited was Pokemon Pokopia, which sold over 4 million units in its launch window and is Nintendo's fastest-selling Pokemon entry on a new platform.

Higher adoption provides Nintendo with the install base justification for the price increase. The company's pricing logic follows a standard hardware lifecycle model: attract early adopters at a subsidized price, then correct toward profitability once the platform is established. A larger-than-expected early install base accelerates that correction timeline.

Nintendo's hybrid platform strategy, combining handheld and docked play with GameChat integration and the expanded Joy-Con 2 feature set, is cited internally as the primary differentiator sustaining adoption against competing platforms including the Steam Deck OLED and cloud gaming alternatives. The Switch 2 vs Steam Deck OLED comparison covers the hardware differentiation in detail.

Console Price US $449.99 to $499.99 +$50 effective September 1 Robust unannounced lineup H2 fiscal year

Adoption Driver Pokemon Pokopia 4M+ sales Higher than Switch 1 rate Value beyond hardware cost Already in market
Unannounced Titles Multiple for H2 2026 Details at appropriate time Zelda Ocarina port speculated Fiscal second half
Stock Performance Down 8% post-announcement Lowest since August 2024 Long-term profitability focus May 11, 2026

The Switch 2 price increase and confirmed H2 software slate together signal Nintendo's commitment to a multi-year platform lifecycle mirroring the original Switch's eight-year run. Furukawa's language around permanent cost increases and long-term profitability focus positions this as a managed transition from subsidized launch pricing to a sustainable revenue model.

The hybrid hardware model, combining 4K DLSS docked output, full-width kickstand, and GameChat with Switch-to-Switch communication, continues to differentiate the platform from both dedicated home consoles and portable PC-class devices. Rhythm Heaven Groove releasing simultaneously on original Switch indicates Nintendo will support the legacy platform in parallel, expanding the addressable software market without fracturing the ecosystem.

Industry-wide, the pricing correction accelerates a broader trend of premium console pricing. Sony's PlayStation 5 Pro at $699.99 and Microsoft's Xbox enforcement investments in titles like Forza Horizon 6 both reflect platform holders moving toward higher margins on hardware and software to offset development cost inflation across the 2026 market.

Which Nintendo Switch 2 bundle secures the lowest price before the September 2026 hike?

Purchase the standard Nintendo Switch 2 at $449.99 or the Mario Kart World bundle at $499.99 before September 1, 2026 at any authorized retailer. See the full Switch 2 pre-order guide for current availability. Nintendo Switch Online Expansion Pack is the recommended subscription for full online feature access and legacy library.

Can Switch 2 owners expect further price increases or a hardware revision in 2026 or 2027?

Furukawa indicated potential additional adjustments if component cost trends persist. No mid-cycle hardware refresh has been announced. Nintendo's historical pattern shows hardware revisions typically arrive 3-4 years into a platform's lifecycle, making a 2027 or 2028 Switch 2 revision more probable than a 2026 change.

What are the confirmed Nintendo Switch 2 games releasing in H2 2026?

Confirmed titles for the second half of 2026 include Fire Emblem Fortune's Weave and multiple unannounced major and non-major titles. Nintendo will disclose details at appropriate times, which typically means a Nintendo Direct event. For the full confirmed 2026 release calendar, see the Nintendo Switch 2 launch games guide.

What is Joy-Con 2 mouse mode and how does it work in Star Fox?

Joy-Con 2 mouse mode uses the right Joy-Con's optical sensor, which detects surface movement when the controller is placed flat. In Star Fox, this maps to aiming and targeting. The feature is optional, with standard analog stick controls remaining the default. Mouse mode is enabled per-game and requires a flat surface for tracking.
